label: Literary Tropes
description: Recognizable narrative devices, structural conventions, or character archetypes that recur across works.

tags.definition: A protagonist who lacks conventional heroic qualities; morally ambiguous or flawed
A protagonist prophesied or destined for a special role
A romantic arc that begins with hostility and transforms into love
The narrative is constructed from letters, diary entries, or documents
A character conceals or fabricates their identity for significant portions of the plot
A character appears to die but is later revealed to be alive
A seductive, dangerous woman whose allure draws others into trouble
A romantic relationship that is opposed by circumstance, society, or family
A group of unrelated individuals who form deep familial bonds
A protagonist of unknown parentage, often with a hidden identity or destiny
A story-within-a-story; an outer narrative contains the central tale
A character's present is shaped by trauma or secrets from their history
The narrative begins in the middle of the action, with backstory revealed later
Property, title, or power passing between generations drives the plot
Three characters in overlapping romantic entanglements
An object or goal that motivates characters but has little intrinsic narrative importance
A wise elder who guides the protagonist before departing or dying
The main character has no living parents; their absence shapes the character's arc
A morally compromised character works toward, achieves, or fails at redemption
The story is told backwards; the ending is revealed first
A former opponent becomes an ally or friend
A romantic or narrative tension that builds gradually over a long arc
A major revelation that recontextualizes what the reader understood
A significant period of in-story time is elided, skipping forward in the narrative
A character who uses cunning, deception, or chaos to achieve goals
The narrator's account is deliberately incomplete, biased, or self-deceiving
The antagonist's worldview has genuine merit; the conflict is morally complex
A step-parent who is actively hostile or cruel to the protagonist
